heading: Civil damages for unauthorized inspection or disclosure of returns and return information
label: 7431 Civil damages for unauthorized inspection or disclosure of returns and return information
notes: Prior ProvisionsA prior section 7431 was renumbered .section 7437 of this title

Amendments2019—Subsec. (e). inserted at end of concluding provisions “The Secretary shall also notify such taxpayer if the Internal Revenue Service or a Federal or State agency (upon notice to the Secretary by such Federal or State agency) proposes an administrative determination as to disciplinary or adverse action against an employee arising from the employee’s unauthorized inspection or disclosure of the taxpayer’s return or return information. The notice described in this subsection shall include the date of the unauthorized inspection or disclosure and the rights of the taxpayer under such administrative determination.”Pub. children.heading: Inspection or disclosure by employee of United States
children.label: 1 Inspection or disclosure by employee of United States
children.text: If any officer or employee of the United States knowingly, or by reason of negligence, inspects or discloses any return or return information with respect to a taxpayer in violation of any provision of section 6103, such taxpayer may bring a civil action for damages against the United States in a district court of the United States.

children.heading: Inspection or disclosure by a person who is not an employee of United States
children.label: 2 Inspection or disclosure by a person who is not an employee of United States
children.text: If any person who is not an officer or employee of the United States knowingly, or by reason of negligence, inspects or discloses any return or return information with respect to a taxpayer in violation of any provision of section 6103 or in violation of section 6104(c), such taxpayer may bring a civil action for damages against such person in a district court of the United States.
which results from a good faith, but erroneous, interpretation of section 6103, or
which is requested by the taxpayer.

children.label: A
children.text: $1,000 for each act of unauthorized inspection or disclosure of a return or return information with respect to which such defendant is found liable, or

children.label: i
children.text: the actual damages sustained by the plaintiff as a result of such unauthorized inspection or disclosure, plus

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a willful inspection or disclosure or an inspection or disclosure which is the result of gross negligence, punitive damages, plus
the costs of the action, plus

in the case of a plaintiff which is described in section 7430(c)(4)(A)(ii), reasonable attorneys fees, except that if the defendant is the United States, reasonable attorneys fees may be awarded only if the plaintiff is the prevailing party (as determined under section 7430(c)(4)).

children.heading: Period for bringing action
children.label: d Period for bringing action
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, an action to enforce any liability created under this section may be brought, without regard to the amount in controversy, at any time within 2 years after the date of discovery by the plaintiff of the unauthorized inspection or disclosure.

children.heading: Notification of unlawful inspection and disclosure
children.label: e Notification of unlawful inspection and disclosure

children.text: subparagraph (B) of , United States Code,section 1030(a)(2) of title 18

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: f Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this section, the terms “inspect”, “inspection”, “return”, and “return information” have the respective meanings given such terms by section 6103(b).

children.heading: Extension to information obtained under section 3406
children.label: g Extension to information obtained under section 3406

children.text: any information obtained under section 3406 (including information with respect to any payee certification failure under subsection (d) thereof) shall be treated as return information, and

any inspection or use of such information other than for purposes of meeting any requirement under section 3406 or (subject to the safeguards set forth in section 6103) for purposes permitted under section 6103 shall be treated as a violation of section 6103.

children.heading: Special rule for information obtained under section 6103(k)(9)
children.label: h Special rule for information obtained under section 6103(k)(9)
children.text: For purposes of this section, any reference to section 6103 shall be treated as including a reference to section 6311(e).
